scenarios.description: Stealthy positive bias on PV60 terminal-voltage telemetry without changing the physical state.
scenarios.expected_effect: The control room sees an apparent PV over-voltage while the true physical state remains nominal.

scenarios.description: Unauthorized command overrides BESS48 dispatch to force a high discharge period.
scenarios.expected_effect: Feeder-head active power decreases and BESS48 SOC drops faster than baseline.
scenarios.notes: Physical effect should be visible in the truth and measured layers.

scenarios.description: Replay of a previous Bus 83 voltage segment onto the measured layer.
scenarios.expected_effect: Bus 83 phase-A telemetry repeats a prior benign segment while underlying truth continues to evolve.
scenarios.notes: Classic replay pattern for zero-day detection work.

scenarios.scenario_name: Coordinated PV Curtailment and Regulator Shift
scenarios.description: Multi-asset campaign curtails PV83 and shifts regulator CREG4A to amplify feeder stress.
scenarios.expected_effect: PV output drops while downstream voltage profile and regulator activity depart from baseline.
scenarios.notes: Campaign-style multi-stage event with coupled cyber and physical signatures.

scenarios.description: A scheduled BESS108 charging command is suppressed, leaving the battery closer to idle during a pre-dawn charging interval.
scenarios.expected_effect: BESS108 charges less than baseline, feeder demand remains slightly higher, and SOC rises more slowly than expected.
scenarios.notes: Representative command suppression case using reusable schedule-aware physical injection.

scenarios.description: A burst-style telemetry degradation event drops feeder-head active-power samples from the measured layer.
scenarios.expected_effect: Measured feeder-head power exhibits a telemetry hole while the physical truth remains continuous.

scenarios.description: A regulator setpoint update is delayed on CREG3A, causing a lag between the cyber event and the physical voltage response.
scenarios.expected_effect: CREG3A tap movement and downstream bus voltage response occur later than the cyber command timestamp suggests.
scenarios.notes: Useful for event-sequence and attack-effect timing validation.
